@Achyut Saxena its loyalty and not royalty. **

THE GAP
1 in 4 COD orders never reaches customers

Cash on Delivery makes up 60 to 65% of all e-commerce orders in India. But 25 to 30% of COD orders end in RTO. Return to Origin. Customer refuses. Wrong address. Changed mind. Fraud.

Each failed delivery costs forward shipping, reverse logistics, blocked inventory. Zero revenue. Prepaid orders only fail 2 to 3% of the time. The gap between COD and prepaid is massive and growing.

Existing solutions cover some damage scenarios. Nothing addresses customer returns, failed deliveries, and fraud comprehensively. Merchants absorb the losses.

We built protection specifically for e-commerce return risks.

Use insights from here as well.

THE GAP
Credit protection hasn't kept up with digital lending

Existing credit protection products are standalone insurance policies sold separately. They require separate applications, underwriting processes, and claim procedures. Borrowers rarely buy them because it's extra work.

When borrowers actually need protection - job loss, medical emergencies, business disruption - the claims process is slow. Months of paperwork. Back and forth with insurance companies. By the time claims get approved, borrowers have already defaulted.

We built credit protection that embeds directly into your lending flow. Protection offered at disbursement. Fast claims tied to verified events. Coverage that works for salaried employees and variable income borrowers.

Coverage that protects borrowers when life disrupts their income. Built to embed directly into your lending flow.

Hey team, quick thought here. I feel like we’re splitting hairs between Purpose and Positioning right now , both are reading like Positioning statements. They’re heavy on descriptors (“contextual, claims-first, end-to-end…”) and starting to blur together.
Just to ground us:
* Purpose = our North Star. Why we exist. It should be short, emotional, inspiring. Think Walmart’s “Save Money. Live Better.” It doesn’t explain, it just lands.
* Positioning = the what/how. How we show up in the market, what we do, who for, how it creates value. This is where the descriptors belong. Think Walmart’s “We are a people-led, tech-powered, omnichannel retailer…”
For Assurekit, that means:
* Purpose should be stripped down to one inspiring truth, e.g. “ protection and benefits for modern life"
* Positioning can carry the detail, e.g. “We are a partner-led, people-first platform that enables brands to build modern, claim-first protection customers actually use and trust.”
This way, we keep one line sharp and emotional (Purpose) and let Positioning do the heavy lifting.

So here are some options to refine this : (1) We are a partner-led, people-first platform that helps brands build modern protection and benefits customers look forward to  using. (2) We build contextual, claims-first, end-to-end protection for everyday needs (3) We help brands deliver protection that keeps pace with modern life. so customers get benefits they actually use and believe in.

Hey team, quick thought here. I feel like we’re splitting hairs between Purpose and Positioning right now , both are reading like Positioning statements. They’re heavy on descriptors (“contextual, claims-first, end-to-end…”) and starting to blur together.
Just to ground us:
* Purpose = our North Star. Why we exist. It should be short, emotional, inspiring. Think Walmart’s “Save Money. Live Better.” It doesn’t explain, it just lands.
* Positioning = the what/how. How we show up in the market, what we do, who for, how it creates value. This is where the descriptors belong. Think Walmart’s “We are a people-led, tech-powered, omnichannel retailer…”
For Assurekit, that means:
* Purpose should be stripped down to one inspiring truth, e.g. “ protection and benefits for modern life"
* Positioning can carry the detail, e.g. “We are a partner-led, people-first platform that enables brands to build modern, claim-first protection customers actually use and trust.”
This way, we keep one line sharp and emotional (Purpose) and let Positioning do the heavy lifting.
